questions.text: RGB is the industry standard color space for printing.
questions.explanation: CMYK (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Key/Black) is the industry standard color space for printing, while RGB is used for digital screens.

questions.text: There is no difference between seeing and perceiving.
questions.explanation: Seeing is the physical act of light hitting the eyes, while perceiving is the mental process of interpreting and making sense of those visual signals.

questions.text: We perceive shapes as flat objects without depth (either real or implied).
questions.explanation: Human perception naturally seeks to understand depth and volume in objects, even when they are represented on a 2D surface.

questions.text: All variations of the grid system are based on the use of horizontal and vertical lines.
questions.explanation: Grid systems are foundational frameworks in design composed of intersecting horizontal and vertical lines used to structure content.

questions.text: A cell is a rectangular module made up of editable regions.
questions.explanation: In design grids, a cell or module is the individual unit created by the intersection of rows and columns; it is not typically defined as being 'made up of editable regions'.

questions.text: ________________ is an area of study that investigates the transmission of ideas and information through visual forms and symbols.
questions.explanation: Visual communication is the practice of using visual elements to convey messages and information.

questions.text: According to the Gutenberg diagram, the reader’s interaction with the text begins in the ________________ quadrant of the page.
questions.explanation: The Gutenberg diagram suggests that Western readers typically begin scanning a page from the top-left corner, known as the primary optical area.

questions.text: The term ________________ refers to the main text of a published document or advertisement and is usually the most abundant source of content on a page.
questions.explanation: Body copy is the main text of any piece of content, excluding headlines, subheadlines, or captions.

questions.text: The law of ________________ states that objects are more likely to be perceived as “belonging together” when they are positioned close to one another.
questions.explanation: The Gestalt Law of Proximity states that items placed near each other are perceived as a group or related unit.

questions.text: The principle of ________________ encourages designers to position objects that belong together along a common edge or implied line.
questions.explanation: Alignment is the placement of visual elements so they line up along common axes, creating order and organization.

questions.text: The area between the arrows in the following figure represents (arrows point to the height of the capital letter 'I'):
questions.explanation: Cap height is the height of a capital letter above the baseline for a particular typeface.

questions.text: What is an organic shape?

choices.label: A
choices.text: one that is created or drawn by a graphic designer

choices.label: B
choices.text: one that resembles an object in the natural world

choices.label: C
choices.text: one that includes lots of color and detail

questions.explanation: Organic shapes are irregular or asymmetrical and often found in nature, as opposed to geometric shapes.

questions.text: In RGB color space, a tertiary color can be derived by mixing which of the following:
questions.explanation: In digital (additive) color, mixing a primary (Green) and a secondary (Yellow) results in a tertiary color such as lime green.

questions.text: The study of fonts and typefaces includes which of the following?
questions.explanation: Typography study encompasses all aspects of characters, including their style, size, casing, and basic design category (serif/sans serif).

questions.text: Which of the following font styles is not recommended to be used in academic writing?
questions.explanation: Outlining characters makes them difficult to read and is considered unprofessional in academic and formal documents.

questions.text: Which of the following font formats were involved in the historical 'font wars'?
questions.explanation: The 'Font Wars' refers to the competition between companies (Adobe, Microsoft, Apple) and their respective font technologies: PostScript, TrueType, and eventually OpenType.

questions.text: What is the size of a digital audio file with the following specifications: duration = 15000 ms, sample rate = 44.1 KHz, sample size = 8 bit, type = Stereo (S)?
questions.explanation:
Calculation: Size = (Duration in s) \times (Sample Rate in Hz) \times (Sample Size in bytes) \times (Channels). 
15s \times 44100 \times (8/8) \times 2 = 1,323,000 bytes. 
1,323,000 / 1,048,576 ≈ 1.26 MB.

questions.text: In digital audio, when the value of each sample is rounded to the nearest integer, this process is called ________________.
questions.explanation: Quantization is the process of mapping continuous infinite values to a smaller set of discrete finite values, such as rounding analog voltage levels to integers.

questions.text: A ________________ is a family of graphic characters, often with many type sizes and styles.
questions.explanation: A typeface is a set of design features for letters and other characters (e.g., Arial). A font is a specific instance of that typeface (e.g., 12pt Arial Bold).

questions.text: The arrow in the following figure indicates to the :
questions.explanation: Because the space inside the 'D' is entirely completely enclosed by the strokes of the letter, it is specifically referred to as a closed counter.

questions.text: __________ is a technique in which a series of progressively different graphics are used on each frame of movie film.
questions.explanation: Cel animation is the traditional technique where each frame is drawn by hand on transparent sheets (cels). This allows characters to be moved over static backgrounds frame-by-frame.

questions.text: __________ is the color system used in TV monitors.
questions.explanation: TV and computer monitors use light-emitting pixels that combine red, green, and blue light (RGB) to create colors. This is known as the **additive color system** because light is added to a black background to create color.

questions.text: The file size of a 10-second recording sampled at 20 kHz, 16-bit stereo (two tracks) would be about:
questions.explanation:
To calculate audio file size: \( \text{Sample Rate} \times \text{Sample Size (in bytes)} \times \text{Channels} \times \text{Time} \).
- Sample Rate: 20,000 Hz
- Sample Size: 16 bits = 2 bytes
- Channels: 2 (stereo)
- Time: 10 seconds

\( 20,000 \times 2 \times 2 \times 10 = 800,000 \text{ bytes} \approx 800 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: __________ places the highest performance demand on computer and its memory and storage.
questions.explanation: Video requires processing thousands of individual image frames per minute along with synchronized audio, making it far more demanding on CPU, RAM, and disk storage than other media types.

questions.text: Multimedia developers use which of the following to create products such as eLearning programs and mobile apps?
questions.explanation: Authoring tools are specialized software (like Adobe Captivate or Articulate Storyline) designed to help developers organize and edit multimedia elements and add interactivity to create final applications.
Virtual Reality (VR) is often described as an advanced extension of multimedia that provides a highly immersive and interactive simulation of a three-dimensional environment.

questions.text: __________ is the color system used to create color in printing.
questions.explanation: Printing uses the **subtractive color model** (CMYK), where ink pigments absorb (subtract) certain wavelengths of light while reflecting others. Combining all primary inks results in black.

questions.text: MIDI is:
questions.explanation: Musical Instrument Digital Interface (MIDI) is a communication protocol that stores performance instructions (like pitch and volume) rather than actual recorded audio data. Therefore, it is not digitized sound (sampled audio).

questions.text: __________ refers to the number of bits used to represent each sound sample.
questions.explanation: Sample size (also called bit depth) specifies the number of bits used to record each sample. Higher sample sizes result in better quality and a larger dynamic range.

questions.text: Digital audio is:
questions.explanation: Digital audio (sampled sound) is **device independent** because the actual sound waves are recorded as data and will sound essentially the same on any hardware capable of playing that file type.

questions.text: Size of a monophonic digital recording is

choices.label: A
choices.text: always larger than Size of stereo recording

choices.label: B
choices.text: always double than Size of stereo recording

choices.label: C
choices.text: always less than Size of stereo recording

choices.label: D
choices.text: always equal than Size of stereo recording

questions.explanation: A monophonic recording uses a single channel, whereas a stereo recording uses two channels. Assuming all other factors (sampling rate, bit depth, duration) are identical, a mono recording requires half the storage space of a stereo recording, meaning it is always less.

questions.text: The American Standard code for Information Interchange (ASCII) is a
questions.explanation: Standard ASCII is a 7-bit character encoding system, which allows it to represent 128 different characters (including control characters, numbers, and letters). While often stored in 8-bit bytes, the original standard is 7-bit.

questions.text: component Video Uses ............................ to connect the camera to a TV or a monitor
questions.explanation: Component video (specifically YPbPr) splits the video signal into three separate channels (luminance and two color difference signals) carried over three separate cables, usually color-coded red, green, and blue.

questions.text: The file size of a 5-second recording sampled at \(20 \text{ kHz}\), 16-bit stereo (two tracks) would be about:
questions.explanation:
File size calculation: \(\text{Sample Rate} \times \text{Sample Size} \times \text{Channels} \times \text{Time}\).
\(20,000 \text{ Hz} \times 2 \text{ bytes (16-bit)} \times 2 \text{ channels} \times 5 \text{ seconds} = 400,000 \text{ bytes}\).
Converting to KB: \(400,000 / 1,000 = 400 \text{ KB}\).

questions.text: Light reflected from an object through the camera's lens is converted into electronic signal by
questions.explanation: A Charge-Coupled Device (CCD) is an image sensor that converts light photons into an electrical charge, which is then processed into a digital signal.

questions.text: File compression is very important when preparing ............................files for the Web
questions.explanation: For web delivery, file size directly impacts performance and bandwidth costs. Animations, sound files, and videos all require significant storage and must be compressed for efficient online use.

questions.text: A ............................ is a collection of characters of a single size and style belonging to a particular typeface family.
questions.explanation: Historically, a 'font' refers to a specific variation (size, weight, style) within a 'typeface' family. For example, 'Helvetica' is a typeface, while '10pt Helvetica Bold' is a font.

questions.text: Bitmapped images

choices.label: B
choices.text: are not known as paint graphics

choices.label: C
choices.text: a data matrix describing the individual dots of an image

questions.explanation: Bitmapped images, also known as raster graphics, are composed of a data matrix of individual pixels (dots), each with assigned color and transparency values.

questions.text: Anti-aliased text must be used

choices.label: D
choices.text: creates irregular boundaries in titles and headlines

questions.explanation: Anti-aliasing smooths the appearance of jagged edges in digital images by adding intermediate colored pixels along boundaries, effectively creating soft boundaries between colors.

questions.text: ................... is concerned with the visual or aesthetic aspect of the project
questions.explanation: Animation is a visual element that adds aesthetic value and motion to a multimedia project, whereas audio and sound are auditory, and text is primarily content-focused.

questions.text: In ..................., color and intensity signals are mixed into a single carrier wave.
questions.explanation: Composite video combines the chrominance (color) and luminance (intensity/brightness) signals into a single signal transmitted over a single wire.

questions.text: The size of ................... images depends on the display resolution, and hardware and software capabilities.
questions.explanation: Bitmap (raster) images are resolution-dependent; their quality and physical size on screen depend on the resolution and pixel density of the display.

questions.text: To play rich media files created in Adobe's Flash or Apple's QuickTime, which of the following may need to be installed into the browser application?
questions.explanation: Browser plug-ins are additional software components that add specific functionality to a web browser, such as the ability to play proprietary media formats like Flash or QuickTime.

questions.text: A Multilayered DVD technology increases the capacity of current optical technology to ...................
questions.explanation: Standard DVD capacity can be increased using multiple layers and sides. A double-sided, dual-layer DVD (DVD-18) has a maximum storage capacity of approximately 17 GB.

questions.text: ................... are used for headlines and bold statements.
questions.explanation: Sans serif fonts (like Arial or Helvetica) lack the small decorative strokes at the ends of letters, making them appear cleaner and more modern, which is preferred for high-impact headlines.

questions.text: ................... is any combination of text, art, sound, animation, and video delivered to you by computer or other electronic or digitally managed means.
questions.explanation: Multimedia is defined as the integration of multiple forms of media (text, audio, images, animation, video) into a single digital application or presentation.

questions.text: When you allow an end user to control what and when the elements are delivered, what is it called?
questions.explanation: Interactive multimedia gives the user control over the navigation and timing of the content, allowing them to choose what they see or hear and when.

questions.text: Animation is possible because of a biological phenomenon known as ...................
questions.explanation: Persistence of vision is the eye's ability to retain an image for a fraction of a second after it disappears. This allows a rapid sequence of static images to be perceived as continuous motion.

questions.text: Painting and drawing software must support for third-party special effect ________.
questions.explanation: Professional painting and drawing software usually supports third-party plug-ins to extend functionality and add special effects.

questions.text: The safe title area is where the image **will be** affected by overscanning and underscan even in the worst conditions.
questions.explanation: The safe title area is the region of the screen where text and graphics are guaranteed NOT to be affected by overscan (cut off).

questions.text: Which one of the following is typically part of a multimedia specification?
questions.explanation: A multimedia specification or project brief typically begins with the multimedia title and the scope of the project.

questions.text: The standards that ensure that project files are given logical names and stored in folders with logical names are the:
questions.explanation: Filenaming conventions provide a systematic way of naming and organizing files and folders for project management.

questions.text: Adding text to a multimedia project requires using a few different typeface as possible but varying the weight and size of your typeface.
questions.explanation: It is a design best practice to limit the number of different typefaces to maintain visual consistency, using weight and size variations for hierarchy instead.

questions.text: If a multimedia application is considered as a **hypermedia product**, it is very likely that the organizational structure has become:
questions.explanation: Hypermedia allows users to jump between pieces of information via links, creating a non-linear navigation structure.

questions.text: To overcome the ________ problem for the delivery of a multimedia project, the multimedia project must be tested in all delivery platforms.
questions.explanation: Testing across all intended platforms ensures that the project functions correctly regardless of variations in hardware and software (cross-platform compatibility).

questions.text: GIF89a allows multiple images to be put into a single file and then be displayed as:

choices.label: B
choices.text: An object in the Web browser

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an object moving in the screen

choices.label: D
choices.text: A still image in the Web browser

choices.label: E
choices.text: An animation in the Web browser

questions.explanation: The GIF89a format supports animation by storing multiple frames within a single file.

questions.text: To create a smooth transition between two images when morphing, it is important to set numerous:
questions.explanation: Morphing requires setting key points on both the start and end images so the software knows how to transform specific features smoothly.

questions.text: ________ is that stage of a project when the programs are tested to make sure that they meet the objectives of the project.
questions.explanation: The testing phase is where the application is evaluated against requirements and objectives before final delivery.

questions.text: Video codecs are lossy. Therefore, avoid reediting.
questions.explanation: Re-editing and re-compressing lossy video files leads to 'generation loss', where visual quality degrades with each save.

questions.text: CMY is a:
questions.explanation: CMY (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) is a subtractive color model used in printing.

questions.text: The component analog video was never adequate for most MM production.
questions.explanation: Component analog video was actually very high quality and widely used in professional multimedia production before digital standards became dominant.

questions.text: 3D modeling software features must include lathing and ________ features.
questions.explanation: Lathing and extruding are the two primary methods used to create 3D shapes from 2D profiles.

questions.text: With ________ and a scanner, you can convert paper documents into a wordprocessing document on your computer without retyping or rekeying.
questions.explanation: OCR (Optical Character Recognition) software converts images of text into editable text data.

questions.text: Which of the following is a television signal format?
questions.explanation: ATSC (Advanced Television Systems Committee) is the specific digital television signal standard used in North America.

questions.text: In Cel animation, Tweening is a complete process that does NOT need alterations.
questions.explanation: Tweening (inbetweening) often requires manual clean-up and alterations to ensure characters look consistent and maintain correct proportions.

questions.text: When shooting video, utilize closeup and medium shots when possible.
questions.explanation: In multimedia, where playback screens may be small, close-up and medium shots are better for conveying detail and emotion.

questions.text: Which of the following is/are the consideration of a multimedia audio specialist?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sampling rate at which the audio will be delivered.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Locating and selecting suitable music and talent.

choices.label: C
choices.text: How much space is available in your hard disk?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Which authoring system or programming language to use.

questions.explanation: A primary technical concern for an audio specialist is the sampling rate, which determines the frequency range and quality of the digital audio.

questions.text: Which of the following is a good idea when creating titles (text) to be used in video?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fonts for titles should be serif, and bold enough to be easily read.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Do NOT kern your letters too tightly.

choices.label: C
choices.text: When you are laying text onto a dark background, use different color for the text.

choices.label: D
choices.text: If you use underlining or drawn graphics, make sure your lines are only one pixel wide.

questions.explanation: Tight kerning in video can cause letters to bleed together due to low resolution or compression, making text hard to read.

questions.text: Rendering allows the Use of intricate algorithms to apply userabstract effects.
questions.explanation: Rendering is the process where computer algorithms calculate pixels to apply textures, lighting, and effects to a scene.

questions.text: A video signal transmitted with all the signals mixed together and carried on a single cable is called:
questions.explanation: Composite video combines brightness (Luma) and color (Chroma) signals into a single cable, usually via an RCA connector.

questions.text: An eBook that does not offer the user navigational control, forcing the user to read from beginning to end, is an example of a multimedia project that is ________.
questions.explanation: Linear projects progress in a fixed sequence without allowing user-driven navigation.

questions.text: ________ and playback features should enable developers to build part of a project and then test it immediately.
questions.explanation: Multimedia authoring tools usually include internal playback systems to allow for rapid iterative development and testing.

questions.text: Which image file type is the best for CAD programs?
questions.explanation: CAD (Computer-Aided Design) relies on mathematical vector data to represent precise geometric shapes that can be scaled without quality loss.

questions.text: Multimedia is essentially made as a way of:
questions.explanation: At its core, multimedia is a medium for communicating information through various combined formats like text, audio, and video.

questions.text: SECAM has a screen resolution of 625 horizontal lines and is a 50 frames per second.
questions.explanation: SECAM uses 625 lines and 50 fields per second, which equals 25 frames per second.

questions.text: Apple hardware platform is considered by many multimedia developers to be the ONLY hardware equipped to manage both sound and video editing.
questions.explanation: While popular, Apple is not the ONLY platform; Windows-based PCs are also fully capable of professional sound and video editing.

questions.text: The ability to act as a teacher and technical coach to the team is the most important skill a multimedia programmer can bring to a team.
questions.explanation: The most important skill for a programmer is technical expertise in coding and logic. Coaching is a secondary management skill.

questions.text: A package of software applications that might include a Word processors, spreadsheet, database, email, web browser, and presentation applications is called a(n) ________.
questions.explanation: An office suite is a collection of productivity software bundled together.

questions.text: Many digital video sources exist, but getting the rights can be difficult, timeconsuming, and expensive.
questions.explanation: Licensing copyrighted video content often involves complex legal and financial negotiations.

questions.text: Most authoring packages include visual effects such as:
questions.explanation: Fades are standard transition effects found in almost every multimedia authoring package.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT considered as a source of digital images?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Frames extracted from digital video clips

questions.explanation: Screen readers are accessibility tools that convert text to speech or braille; they do not generate digital images.

questions.text: Inverse kinematics is the process of linking objects and defining their relationship and limits.
questions.explanation: Inverse kinematics (IK) defines how joints move in a hierarchy, allowing for realistic skeletal animation.

questions.text: The video technique that allows you to choose a color or range of colors that become transparent, allowing the video image to be visible behind those colors in the overlying image, is known as:

choices.label: B
choices.text: All of the above are appropriate names

questions.explanation: Chroma keying is the process of compositing two images or video streams together based on color hues.

questions.text: Some colors generated by a computer that display fine on an RGB monitor may be illegal for display on an NTSC TV.
questions.explanation: NTSC has a narrower color gamut than computer RGB monitors; saturated colors outside this range are 'illegal' and cause visual artifacts.

questions.text: Which of these is a necessary capability for a multimedia video specialist?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ability to make a video look larger than it really is.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Familiarity with the tools and techniques used for audio production.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ability to incorporate all the sophisticated video effects into a multimedia production.

choices.label: D
choices.text: managing all phases of multimedia production.

questions.explanation: A video specialist must have high expertise in utilizing video production tools and applying visual effects.

questions.text: In which stage a product is revised, based on the continuous feedback received from the client?
questions.explanation: Continuous revision based on feedback is part of the iterative development loop within the Design and Production phase.

questions.text: ____________ authoring tools are best suited for messages with a beginning and an end.
questions.explanation: Time-based authoring tools (like Adobe Animate or Director) use a timeline to organize events, making them ideal for linear presentations with a clear beginning and end.

questions.text: Image editing programs enable the user to Enhance and make composite images.
questions.explanation: Image editing software, such as Adobe Photoshop, provides tools specifically designed for color correction, enhancement, and the layering of multiple images to create composites.

questions.text: Which of these is **a reason** why animation is perceived as motion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An image remains in the eye chemically for a brief time after viewing.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Our mind tries to "connect the dots" by completing perceived actions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The use of darker colors for moving objects is interpreted by the mind as motion.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A sequence of images is read as continuous motion.

choices.label: E
choices.text: All of the above are valid reasons.

questions.explanation: Motion perception in animation relies on several biological and psychological phenomena, including persistence of vision (image lingering in the eye), the phi phenomenon (mind connecting static images), and the rapid sequential display of related images.

questions.text: Which of the following is a color specification format?
questions.explanation: HSB stands for Hue, Saturation, and Brightness. It is a color model/specification format used to define colors, whereas GIF and TIFF are image file formats.

questions.text: What is the file size of a 10 second recording sampled at 20 kHz, 16-bit, two tracks?
questions.explanation:
The formula for digital audio file size is: \( \text{Sample Rate} \times \text{Sample Size in bytes} \times \text{Time} \times \text{Channels} \). 
- Sample Rate: \( 20,000 \text{ Hz} \) 
- Sample Size: \( 16 \text{ bits} = 2 \text{ bytes} \) 
- Time: \( 10 \text{ seconds} \) 
- Channels: \( 2 \) 
Calculation: \( 20,000 \times 2 \times 10 \times 2 = 800,000 \text{ bytes} \).

questions.text: Which of these is likely to be the responsibility of a project manager?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Monitor the overall development and implementation of a project.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Oversee budgets, schedules, creative sessions, and team dynamics.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Acquires the assets used in the project.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Develop extensions to the authoring system.

questions.explanation: A project manager is responsible for the high-level management of the project, which includes financial oversight (budgets), time management (schedules), facilitating collaboration (creative sessions), and managing personnel (team dynamics).

questions.text: .............................. is not a stage of a multimedia project
questions.explanation: The typical stages of a multimedia project include Planning and Costing, Designing and Producing, Testing, and Delivering. Marketing is generally considered a business function outside of the core development lifecycle.

questions.text: When you turn off your computer, which type of memory is not lost?
questions.explanation: Read-Only Memory (ROM) is non-volatile, meaning it retains its contents even when power is lost. RAM is volatile and is wiped when power is turned off. EIDE and SCSI are interface standards for storage devices, not types of memory themselves.

questions.text: For multimedia production, mastering your medium means knowing..............................
questions.explanation: In the context of production, 'mastering the medium' refers to having a deep technical understanding of the hardware and software tools required to create the content.

questions.text: the responsibility of a multimedia designer is NOT

choices.label: B
choices.text: creating budgets and timelines for the project

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensuring the visual consistency of the project

questions.explanation: Creating budgets and timelines is the responsibility of a multimedia project manager, not the designer. The designer focuses on visual layout, structure, and user interface.

questions.text: With .............................. and a scanner, you can convert paper documents into a word-processing document on your computer without retyping or rekeying.
questions.explanation: OCR (Optical Character Recognition) software analyzes the scanned image of a text document and converts the shapes of the characters into editable digital text.

questions.text: Selecting media types for content is the responsibility of
questions.explanation: A multimedia designer is responsible for the overall look and feel of the project, which includes choosing the appropriate media (graphics, audio, video) to convey the message.

questions.text: the ability to quickly learn and understand systems is the most important skill of
questions.explanation: Multimedia programmers must frequently adapt to new software development kits (SDKs), APIs, and authoring tools, making the ability to rapidly learn complex systems a critical skill.

questions.text: .............................. places the highest performance demand on computer and its memory and storage
questions.explanation: Digital video requires significantly more processing power, RAM, and storage space than text, audio, or graphics due to the high data rate of high-resolution image frames played in sequence.

questions.text: .............................. has a screen resolution of 625 horizontal lines and a scan rate of 25 frames per second
questions.explanation: The PAL (Phase Alternating Line) analog television standard uses 625 lines and 25 frames per second. NTSC uses 525 lines at approximately 30 frames per second.

questions.text: .............................. standard allows for transmission of data to computers and for new Advanced TV (ATV) interactive services
questions.explanation: ATSC (Advanced Television Systems Committee) standards provide for digital television transmission, which includes support for high-definition video, multichannel audio, and ancillary data services.

questions.text: .............................. occurs when an image is larger than the standard TV screen
questions.explanation: Overscan is a situation where the edges of the broadcast image are cropped by the television display's bezel, effectively making the source image 'larger' than the viewable area.

questions.text: while shooting a video, good .............................. is important
questions.explanation: High-quality video production requires attention to all aspects, including controlled lighting, clear sound, and the absence of unwanted noise.

questions.text: .............................. is an object moving across or into or out of the screen
questions.explanation: Animation is the process of creating the illusion of motion through the rapid display of a sequence of static images.

questions.text: .............................. is possible because of a biological phenomenon known as persistence of vision and a psychological phenomenon called phi
questions.explanation: Persistence of vision (the eye retaining an image for a fraction of a second) and the Phi phenomenon (the brain perceiving continuous motion between separate objects) are the foundations of how we perceive animation.

questions.text: MIDI Multimedia sound records
questions.explanation: MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) does not record actual audio waveforms; instead, it records digital instructions (notes, velocity, pitch) similar to musical notation that tell a synthesizer how to play sound.

questions.text: .............................. represent the amplitude (or loudness) of sound at a discrete point in time
questions.explanation: In digital audio recording, 'samples' are the individual measurements of the sound wave's amplitude taken at specific intervals.

questions.text: What is the size of a bitmap image given an image resolution of 50*20 pixels and a color depth of 24 bits?
questions.explanation:
Calculation: \( \text{Pixels} \times \text{Color Depth} \).
Total pixels = \( 50 \times 20 = 1,000 \text{ pixels} \).
Total bits = \( 1,000 \times 24 \text{ bits} = 24,000 \text{ bits} \).
Converting to Bytes: \( 24,000 / 8 = 3,000 \text{ Bytes} \).

questions.text: .............................. provide tools for bitmap and vector graphics destined for Web presentations
questions.explanation: Macromedia/Adobe Director was a powerful multimedia authoring tool that allowed for the integration of various media types, including bitmap and vector graphics, for distribution on the web via Shockwave.

questions.text: In large-size headlines, it is suggested to

choices.label: A
choices.text: vary the size of a font in proportion to the importance of the message

choices.label: C
choices.text: vary the weight and size of your typeface

questions.explanation: For large-size headlines, 'kerning' or adjusting the spacing between individual letters is essential to ensure readability and professional appearance, as default spacing often looks uneven at large scales.
Virtual Reality (VR) is considered an advanced extension of multimedia that incorporates immersive, interactive 3D environments.

questions.text: When you provide a structure of linked elements through which the user can navigate, interactive multimedia becomes ..............................
questions.explanation: Hypermedia is a non-linear medium of information that includes graphics, audio, video, plain text, and hyperlinks to create a structured path for navigation.
